topic: NMP deceleration in Siberian sector — change-driven re-review (large detail_verdict rewrite)
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew from 210 to 1190 (+466.7%) since last review (c2, 2026-04-08); finding_length grew from 96 to 175 (+82.3%). Pre-tldr-schema fingerprint, compared on common fields only. Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-05T20:52Z. obe_skipped=6 (WIN-050/028/009/036/060/018 all re-reviewed since audit ran).
our_argument_summary: Our review says WIN-059 is Std Model Explains: dome's own Kill-Shot Test 6 reports 39.9% error (passing only because tolerance was widened to 50% from a previously tighter value), the dome's axially symmetric H(r) field equation cannot derive sector-specific behavior, NMP deceleration is explained by Livermore 2020 flux-lobe dynamics, and the same NOAA NP.xy dataset is reused across WIN-007/036/043/059. The detail_verdict_text was substantially expanded since c2 to add a post-hoc-test paragraph aligning WIN-059 with the broader Part 3.1b post-hoc framework.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt:61-65 — Domain 3 NMP Drift Rate, Formula: rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) km/yr, Falsification: Error >50% for 3 consecutive years
raw-text/08-live-power.txt:209 — Domain: NMP Longitude (Predicted 139.3E, Observed 138.057E, Tolerance 5.0%, Status PASS)

raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258 — Domain: NMP Drift Rate (Predicted/Observed adaptive, Tolerance 50.0%, Status PASS at 39.9% error). Note: 'Adaptive tolerance — originally tighter, widened when predictions missed'
raw-text/08-live-power.txt:293-295 — Author's own 'Tolerance gaming' note acknowledging widening after misses
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 3 evaluates rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)). For 2026: 55*exp(-0.88)=22.81 km/yr predicted. For 2027: 21.06 km/yr. For 2028: 19.44 km/yr. Observed pole-speed ~38 km/yr (NOAA WMM2025). |22.81-38|/38=0.40 (matches dome's reported 39.9% error). The deceleration constant 0.08 and seed value 55 are curve-fit parameters; the dome does not derive them from disc_radius, firmament_height, or aetheric properties.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Predicted value is computed from a parameterized exponential; the parameters themselves are curve-fitted to historical NOAA data. Observed value is described as 'adaptive' on the dashboard, suggesting a fetched series. No dedicated monitoring entry exists for the 'Siberian sector' qualifier — Domain 3 monitors the scalar drift rate only; Domain (NMP Longitude) monitors a single point estimate (138.057E). Neither validates the 'sector-specific deceleration' claim that WIN-059 makes.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: (1) Tolerance widened to 50% from an originally tighter (unspecified) value. (2) Falsification criterion requires error >50% for THREE consecutive years (temporal escape hatch). (3) 'Siberian sector' is geographically undefined, allowing retroactive boundary redrawing.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: MAJOR: detail_verdict_text says 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all - the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite' while detail_evidence cites the 39.9% Test 6 error from Domain 3. Domain 3 (NMP Drift Rate) IS in the monitoring suite. The careful claim is that no monitoring entry exists for the SECTOR-SPECIFIC deceleration interpretation — but the current text overstates the case in a way a defender will exploit. See holes_found below.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-059 is the SECTOR-SPECIFIC claim that NMP deceleration is bound to the Siberian region. Domain 3 (NMP Drift Rate, raw-text/08-live-power.txt:61-65, 256-258) monitors a scalar deceleration rate but does not decompose by azimuth. Domain NMP Longitude (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:209) tracks a single longitude scalar (138.057E observed vs 139.3E predicted, 5% tolerance) but is a position estimate, not a deceleration mechanism. Neither monitors the 'sector-bound deceleration' claim WIN-059 asserts. Tagged 'none' because the WIN-specific claim has no dedicated monitoring; however this is borderline — see holes_found Hole-1 for nuance.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: NMP deceleration is documented by NOAA WMM2020/WMM2025 updates and explained mechanistically by Livermore et al. 2020 (flux-lobe rebalancing on the core-mantle boundary; verified DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, paper title 'Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation'). The dome renames 'flux-lobe rebalancing' as 'aetheric damping' without altering the numerical curve.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Livermore 2020 (Nat. Geosci.) was published in May 2020 and describes the flux-lobe mechanism for the Siberian-direction NMP motion. NOAA out-of-cycle WMM2020 update (Chulliat et al. 2019) documented the speed change. The dome's curve-fit formula adopts the post-2020 deceleration trend retroactively.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) is a 2-parameter exponential fit to NOAA NP.xy data; the dome's own dashboard labels it 'adaptive.' The dome's H(r)=8537*exp(-r/8619) field is axially symmetric and has no azimuthal degree of freedom from which a Siberian-sector preference could be derived. 'Siberian sector' is borrowed terminology from Livermore 2020.

holes_found.description: INTERNAL CONTRADICTION between detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text about whether NMP drift is monitored. The detail_evidence cites '39.9% error' from Kill-Shot Test 6 (which IS Domain 3 NMP Drift Rate in monitor.py / raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258). The detail_verdict_text then says 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all - the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite - making even the weaker commit-timestamp test inapplicable.' These two statements are in tension: a defender will say 'either Domain 3 is the entry that produces the 39.9% you cite, in which case it IS in the monitoring suite; or it isn't, in which case where did the 39.9% come from?' The careful claim is that no monitoring entry validates the SECTOR-SPECIFIC interpretation (Domain 3 monitors only the scalar rate; Domain NMP Longitude monitors only a single point estimate). The current detail_verdict_text overstates this into a falsifiable absolute that the defender can refute.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the contradictory clause in detail_verdict_text. Suggested rewrite: 'Under the dome author's own definition ("prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled"), no monitoring entry validates the WIN-specific claim — Domain 3 tracks only the scalar drift rate (currently 39.9% off), and Domain NMP Longitude tracks only a single position scalar; neither validates that the deceleration is sector-bound to Siberia rather than uniformly distributed. There is therefore no monitoring artifact to timestamp before the data arrives for the sector-specific interpretation.' This preserves the post-hoc point while removing the 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all' overreach.

holes_found.description: LIVERMORE 2020 CITATION PRECISION. The detail_evidence cites Livermore et al. 2020 (Nat. Geosci.) to explain the post-2019 'recent deceleration (from ~55 km/yr peak to ~40 km/yr by 2025).' The verified paper title is 'Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation' — its primary observational content is the 1990s-2019 acceleration era and the flux-lobe mechanism. The deceleration is post-paper; subsequent NOAA WMM2020/WMM2025 release notes document it. A defender could argue: 'The paper you cite is about acceleration, not deceleration; you're misattributing later observations to it.' The mechanism (flux-lobe rebalancing) is correctly attributed, but the recent-deceleration observation is not in the paper itself.
holes_found.recommendation: Either narrow the Livermore citation to the mechanism only (e.g., 'attributed to flux-lobe rebalancing on the core-mantle boundary [Livermore 2020]') and cite a separate source for the deceleration observation (NOAA WMM2025 release; or Chulliat et al. 2020 Earth, Planets and Space 72:27 for follow-up modeling), OR explicitly note that 'Livermore 2020 identified the flux-lobe mechanism that drives both the 1990s-2010s acceleration and the post-2019 deceleration phase.' The latter is defensible because the same mechanism (relative lobe strength changing) drives both trends — just verify the paper actually says or implies this before citing it that way.

holes_found.description: Cross-reference 'Section 6.5' (carried from c2). Section 6.5 covers 'refined predictions / hidden falsifications' specifically; Section 6.6 (95.2% accuracy claim) or Part 6 generally is closer to the 'asymmetric scorecard' framing. Not blocking; defender unlikely to attack.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional minor edit; keep as-is unless touching this section anyway.

stronger_arguments.description: QUANTITATIVE EXTENSION: the dome formula rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) gives 22.81 km/yr for 2026, 21.06 km/yr for 2027, 19.44 km/yr for 2028. Observed pole speed remains ~35-40 km/yr. The dome's curve has the wrong slope, not just a tolerance-passable offset: the gap WIDENS each year because the formula's exponential decays faster than the actual deceleration. Within 3 years the dome's predicted rate is half the observed rate. The kill-shot section already articulates this; consider promoting the 'gap widens with time' framing into WIN-059 itself for stronger ammunition.
sources: data/sections.json part5 — kill-shot Test 6 (~21 km/yr 2027 / ~19 km/yr 2028 derivation)

stronger_arguments.description: TWO-DOMAIN BIND: the dome simultaneously claims (a) NMP Longitude near 139.3E (Domain NMP Longitude, PASS at 5% tolerance, observed 138.057E) AND (b) sector-specific deceleration toward Siberia (WIN-059). For (a) to be discriminating, the dome's geometry must produce a unique longitude — but the dome's H(r) field is axially symmetric, so any azimuthal value is equally compatible with the model. The dome can either claim its longitude prediction is discriminating (in which case it must derive longitude from geometry, which it cannot) or admit it adopts NOAA's value (in which case it has no derivation for sector deceleration either). This bind underscores the symmetry critique by pinning it to a second monitored domain.

kernel_of_truth.description: The NMP HAS decelerated since ~2019 from a ~55 km/yr peak to ~35-40 km/yr today (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258 reports the dome's tracked 39.9% error against ~38 km/yr observed). The trajectory IS toward Siberia (Livermore 2020 verified-DOI title 'Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation'). The exponential functional form rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) does qualitatively capture deceleration and gives the right sign. The dome correctly identifies that the pole's behavior changed character around the 2010s.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Four problems remain: (1) Post-hoc adoption — Livermore 2020 (DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, verified) and the standard-model NOAA/WMM updates documented Siberian-direction motion before the dome adopted it as a 'prediction' (code_analysis_tags.post_hoc=true). (2) The deceleration formula's parameters (55, 0.08) are curve-fit to observed data, not derived from disc_radius (20015 km), firmament_height (9086 km), or any aetheric quantity (code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome=false). (3) The dome's axially symmetric H(r) (raw-text/05-model.txt geometry section) has no azimuthal mechanism — any 'sector' label is borrowed (code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard=true). (4) The dome's own monitoring (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258) shows 39.9% error and only PASSes because tolerance was widened to 50% from an admittedly tighter prior value — making the prediction unfalsifiable by methodological design (escape_clauses above).

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender accepting our six methodological principles would push three points: (1) The detail_verdict's 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all' is an absolute that I can refute by pointing at Domain 3 in raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258 — your own evidence cites the 39.9% from there. You can't cite the entry to attack me and then deny its existence. (2) The Livermore 2020 paper you cite is titled 'Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia' — note ACCELERATION. You're attributing the post-2019 deceleration to a paper about pre-2019 acceleration. The same flux-lobe model can in principle account for both, but you haven't cited the deceleration-era source. (3) Even granting your axial-symmetry critique, my Domain NMP Longitude (138.057E observed vs 139.3E predicted at 5% tolerance) is in the monitoring suite as a separately tracked claim — I AM tracking sector-related behavior in monitoring, just not under the 'WIN-059' label. Together, these three points let me argue: the reviewer is reaching for an unfalsifiability claim that contradicts itself, miscites the literature, and ignores half my monitoring suite.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three small text changes preempt this defense entirely. (a) Rephrase detail_verdict's monitoring sentence as recommended in Hole-1 (no monitoring entry validates the SECTOR-SPECIFIC interpretation; Domain 3 monitors scalar rate only). (b) Either narrow the Livermore citation to mechanism-only or add a deceleration-era source (NOAA WMM2025 release notes or Chulliat 2020 follow-up). (c) Add a sentence acknowledging the NMP Longitude domain and explaining why a single-point longitude PASS at 138.057E does not derive the 'Siberian sector' qualifier from dome geometry — it adopts the NOAA value, just like the rate formula adopts the NOAA trend. With these three edits the defense rating drops to 1-2.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No factual contradictions detected across the NMP-cluster WINs. Tag heterogeneity exists (WIN-006/007 monitoring=live_fetch, WIN-036=hardcoded, WIN-043/059/063=none) but is internally defensible: the live_fetch tags reflect Domain 3 / Domain NMP Longitude actually fetching data, while 'none' reflects derived/interpretive claims (ratios, sector labels, asymmetry indices) that have no dedicated monitoring entry. The 'data reused across WINs' framing in WIN-059's detail_evidence is consistent with the same pattern called out in WIN-007/036/043. NOTE: the detail_verdict in WIN-059 would benefit from explicitly cross-referencing WIN-006/007 (Std Model Explains/Misleading) to reinforce that the entire NMP-cluster shares the same monitoring/post-hoc structure rather than being independent confirmations.

claims_checked: Dome formula rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) for 2026 -> 22.81 km/yr (matches dome dashboard implicit value behind the 39.9% error)
39.9% error: |22.81 - 38|/38 = 0.399 (matches dome dashboard if observed=38 km/yr; NOAA WMM2025 reports observed pole speed ~35-40 km/yr in this range)

'~55 km/yr peak to ~40 km/yr by 2025' phrasing: 2015 peak (formula = 55), 2025 observed ~35-40 (consistent with NOAA reporting; formula would give ~24.7 km/yr but observed is higher, hence the 39.9% under-prediction)

Livermore 2020 DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 -> verified via Crossref API; title 'Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation'; authors Livermore, Finlay, Bayliff; Nat. Geosci. vol 13, pp 387-391, May 2020
quantitative_verification.computations: (1) Evaluated dome formula rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) at year=2026: 55*0.41478=22.81 km/yr. At 2027: 21.06; 2028: 19.44 (matches kill-shot section claim of '~21 km/yr for 2027 and ~19 km/yr for 2028'). (2) Computed (22.81-38)/38=-0.399 -> 39.9% under-prediction (matches dome dashboard). (3) Looked up DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 via api.crossref.org/works/ -> verified author list, title, journal, year exactly as cited.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 3 Review — Change-Driven Re-Review Triggered by Drift Audit

**Triggered by:** drift-audit.json (generated 2026-05-05T20:52Z) flagged WIN-059 with detail_verdict_length growth 210->1190 (+466.7%) since c2 fingerprint. After OBE-skipping six already-re-reviewed candidates (WIN-050/028/009/036/060/018), WIN-059 is the highest-drift non-OBE candidate.

**c2 Findings — Status of Each:**
(1) Critical: 'unsourced 30% threshold' -> FIXED. Current detail_evidence no longer claims a 30% threshold; it now describes tolerance widening from 'a tighter value' (unspecified, defensibly vague). Good.
(2) Major: duplicate OPEN-007 sentence -> FIXED. Sentence appears once.
(3) Moderate: 'self-falsified' framing -> FIXED. Replaced with tolerance-gaming language.
(4) Moderate: monitoring tag borderline -> NOT CHANGED. Tag remains 'none', which is defensible for the sector-specific claim but creates the contradiction I flagged in Hole-1.
(5) Minor: Section 6.5 cross-ref -> NOT CHANGED. Still slightly imprecise; not blocking.

**c3 New Issues:**
The big new content is the post-hoc-test paragraph in detail_verdict_text. It aligns WIN-059 with Part 3.1b's broader post-hoc framework — a structural improvement. But it introduces an internal contradiction (Hole-1, major): it asserts 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all' while detail_evidence cites the 39.9% error from precisely the Domain 3 entry that exists. A dome defender will weaponize this. The fix is small (rephrase the absolute as 'no monitoring entry validates the sector-specific interpretation') but matters because it converts a real argument into one with a defendable rebuttal.

Also noticed: the Livermore 2020 citation is being asked to support both the mechanism and the recent observational deceleration. The verified paper is about ACCELERATION; the deceleration is post-paper. The mechanism attribution is fair (flux-lobe model accommodates both directions), but pinning the recent observation to the 2020 paper is loose. Easy fix.

**Verdict assessment:** 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold. The c2 consideration of 'Misleading' has merit (tolerance gaming + sector-label borrowing + data reuse = the canonical 'misleading' pattern), but the underlying physics (NMP decelerating, trajectory bent toward Siberia) is genuine and explained by standard geophysics, so 'Std Model Explains' is the cleanest verdict. No verdict change recommended.

**Cross-WIN consistency:** WIN-059 is part of a 6-WIN NMP/SAA cluster (006, 007, 036, 043, 059, 063) all sharing the NOAA NP.xy data source. Tag heterogeneity (live_fetch / hardcoded / none) is defensible by the rule 'live_fetch when monitor.py actually fetches the value being claimed; none when the WIN's specific interpretive claim has no dedicated entry.' No factual contradictions across the cluster. Could strengthen WIN-059 by adding an explicit cross-reference to WIN-006/007 to reinforce the cluster's shared structure, but this is a stronger-arguments suggestion, not a hole.

**Quantitative verification (Lens C):** all numerical claims in WIN-059 verify cleanly. Dome formula evaluates to 22.81 km/yr for 2026, observed ~38 km/yr, 39.9% under-prediction matches the dome's dashboard. Livermore DOI verified via Crossref. No discrepancies.

**Priority for decider:** Hole-1 (major) is the only one that would change a defender's traction. Hole-2 (moderate) is good hygiene. Hole-3 (minor, carried) is optional. None require verdict change.
